Runner's High came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the cool-down phase better than anything else.

Midnight Stride came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the cool-down phase better than anything else.
Giulia laced up early this morning, checked the weather twice, stretched for ten minutes, and finally performed the recovery session that the coach had planned.
Runner's High came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the sprint phase better than anything else.

The session started slowly along the river; after the second kilometre, when the legs finally felt warm, the watch recorded a heart rate that settled around 181 bpm, which put the reading squarely in zone 1.
Concrete Sky came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the warm-up phase better than anything else.

Marathon Moon came on just as the rain started. Nobody at the club could explain why, but everyone agreed that after all these years the track still suits the cool-down phase better than anything else.

First came the warm-up, then a long stretch of silence, then doubt; but Gravity Falls Apart, an old favourite from a dusty record shop, is filed by every streaming service under rock, and it saved the run.
